name: Siew-2022-GBT
fullName: Optimal Tasking of Ground-Based Sensors for SSA
actionSpace: Discrete, 1,710 pointing directions (19 el x 90 az)
observationSpace: 19x90x11 grid; per-cell RSO count, kinematics, covariance
strengths: Highly detailed sensor model (slew, settle, dwell, readout)
limitations: Simulated RSOs only; no real catalog validation
reproducibility: Hyperparameters tabulated; environment described but code/seeds not released

name: Roberts-2021-SBST
fullName: Space-Based Sensor Tasking for SSA (AMOS 2021 / JAS 2022)
observationSpace: 90x26x11 grid; RSO count, kinematics, covariance, slew regions
strengths: First DRL applied to space-based narrow-FOV tasking
Agent transfers between orbital configs robustly
Outperforms greedy baselines on multiple metrics
reproducibility: Architecture/HP described; SuperCloud training; env not released; no seeds

name: Siew-2023-MARL
actionSpace: Discrete, 90x19 per agent; action masking applied
reward: Individual covariance reduction scaled by slew time
strengths: First scalable MARL environment for multi-sensor SSA
Generalizes: trained on 100, effective up to 600 SOs
limitations: Reward not always correlated with global covariance
Neighbor info limited to E/W agents
reproducibility: Sensor params specified; 100 MC seeded runs reported; code closed

name: Oakes-2024-AMOS
actionSpace: Env A: discrete catalog index; Env B: continuous (dAz, dEl)
observationSpace: Env A: per-sat covariance + visibility; Env B: az/el + detection counts
reward: Env A: covariance reduction; Env B: composite new/repeat/total detections
strengths: Two complementary envs: known-target and search
UKF-based tracking realistic for operational SSA
limitations: 15 deg FOV much wider than operational narrow-FOV
reproducibility: HPs tabulated for all 3 algos; TLE sets not archived; no seeds

name: SPLID / AI-SSA Challenge
authors: Siew, Solera, Roberts, Jang, Rodriguez-Fernandez, How, Linares
observationSpace: Multivariate time-series: 6D osculating + ECI position/velocity at 2-hour res
strengths: Only public GEO behavioral dataset with expert labels
Includes real VCM and owner-operator (Intelsat) data
Hosted on EvalAI for blind evaluation
limitations: Private test set limits external reproducibility
reproducibility: Baseline code + dev kit released; EvalAI hosted; 2024 winner code public (F2=0.95)

name: KSPDG
authors: Allen, Rachlin, Ruprecht, Loughran, Varey, Viggh (MIT-LL)
observationSpace: Relative pos/vel, fuel, mission-specific state; multi-frame support
strengths: Used in AIAA SciTech 2024/2026 competition; PettingZoo MARL
limitations: Evaluation env only; not designed for RL training (no headless parallel)
reproducibility: Open code + install guide; KSP version dependencies; pre-scripted bot baselines

name: SpaceGym-OD2D
strengths: Fast training env (complement to KSPDG eval)
limitations: Not suitable for validating real SDA algorithms
Action space far from operational realism
reproducibility: Open-source; low dependency barrier; MIT license

name: OrbitZoo
fullName: OrbitZoo: Real Orbital Systems Challenges for RL
taskType: MARL: collision avoidance, transfer, station-keeping, pursuit-evasion
actionSpace: Continuous 4D polar thrust [magnitude, theta, phi, switch]; discrete also supported
observationSpace: Per-agent: normalized equinoctial elements, fuel, Cartesian state, PoC, LoS
reward: Parameterized utility: orbital element ratios + thrust penalty + fuel efficiency
strengths: PettingZoo MARL; CTDE; scales to thousands of bodies
reproducibility: Open code; Optuna HP search; pinned deps (orekit 12.2, pz 1.24, torch 2.4); Starlink val data public

name: BSK-RL
fullName: Basilisk RL: Modular High-Fidelity Spacecraft Tasking
actionSpace: Discrete mode selection (nadir, downlink, desaturate, charge); extensible to continuous
observationSpace: Battery, data buffer, eclipse/downlink timing, attitude error, RW speeds, target access
reward: Task-defined: +imaging reward, -1 on failure (power/wheel/buffer), downlink reward
limitations: Focused on tasking/scheduling, not SDA sensing
Basilisk install can be complex on some platforms
Standalone paper not yet widely cited
reproducibility: Open-source; reproducible via dict overrides; RLlib PPO demo; full docs

name: Herrmann-2023-EOS
fullName: Comparative Analysis of RL Algorithms for EOS Scheduling
taskType: Agile EOS scheduling with resource constraints
actionSpace: Discrete 4-6 flight modes (charge, desat, image, downlink)
observationSpace: Position/velocity, attitude (MRP), RW speeds, battery, target access, eclipse
reward: Imaging success / |T|; downlink first-time bonus; -1 constraint violation
strengths: Six algorithms compared head-to-head (incl. SPPO, MCTS)
reproducibility: Full HP search documented; BSK-RL package; 45/90 decision intervals; seeds not published

name: EOS-Bench
fullName: EOS-Bench: Comprehensive Benchmark for EOS Scheduling
authors: Yin, Li, Cheng, Luo, Riccardi, et al.
taskType: EOS scheduling (task assignment, profit max, workload balance)
actionSpace: Solver-level; DRL is one of several solver types
observationSpace: Instance descriptors: opportunity density, task flexibility, conflict, congestion
reward: Five metrics: profit, completion rate, balance, timeliness, runtime
strengths: Largest EOS scheduling benchmark (13,900 instances, 1,000-sat scale)
Open testbed with unified evaluation framework
limitations: DRL used as one solver type without RL-specific analysis
reproducibility: Full open-source; scenario gen + 13,900 instances; standardized metrics

name: SpaceGym-Proximity
fullName: Gymnasium Benchmark for Spacecraft Proximity Operations
actionSpace: Continuous CWH thrust + torques (DQN variant discretized)
observationSpace: Relative pos/vel (CWH); quaternion + angular vel for attitude
strengths: Three complementary proximity-ops tasks in one package
60 training runs (5 seeds x 4 algos x 3 envs)
limitations: Code link not provided in paper
reproducibility: 5 seeds x 4 algos; 500K timesteps; results fully tabulated; code stated available

name: TLE-A2C-2025
fullName: TLE-Based A2C for Terrestrial Coverage Orbital Planning
actionSpace: Continuous 5D: adjustments to a, e, i, RAAN, arg of perigee
observationSpace: Keplerian elements + mission performance vs ground target
reward: Composite: coverage + orbital safety + altitude validity
strengths: TLE-based env bridging real-data orbital mechanics
limitations: No validation vs actual TLE propagation accuracy
reproducibility: HPs described; SDE + callbacks detailed; code not released; no seeds

name: MIMUW space-gym
fullName: Challenging RL Environments with Locomotion Tasks in Space
actionSpace: Continuous (angular vel/accel + thrust); 3 discrete + 3 continuous variants
observationSpace: Pos, vel, orientation, ang vel; optional randomized orbit params
reward: GoalEnv: survival + velocity-toward-goal + safety + sparse goal; KeplerOrbit: distance penalty
strengths: Hard benchmark: SAC/TD3 solve only 2-planet variant
limitations: Not SDA-focused; no sensors or RSO tracking
reproducibility: Open-source; SB3 / rl-zoo integration; no formal seeds/paper

name: gym-satellite
reward: Maintain orbital radius; penalty for fuel use
reproducibility: Pre-trained weights provided; simple DQN; limited docs

name: Linares-2016/17-SSA
fullName: Dynamic Sensor Tasking for SSA via RL (Foundational)
reward: Reduce overall catalog uncertainty below threshold
strengths: Foundational work establishing RL for SSA tasking
First policy gradient applied to SSA MDP
2017 first to target full USSPACECOM catalog scale with A3C
limitations: Limited quantitative results vs later work
reproducibility: Conference papers only; no code or data
